title: ecliptica alba var. parviflora
intro: Ecliptica alba var. parviflora, also known as small-flowered eclipta, is a plant species in the daisy family, native to Asia, with its leaves and flowers used in traditional medicine. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, derived from its sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids. Ecliptica alba var. parviflora is used to treat various health conditions, such as fever, rheumatism, and skin diseases, due to its health benefits, including reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, and it is also used as a topical application or added to cosmetics for its skin-soothing properties.
